action_plan.title: Strengthen SQL Project Evidence
action_plan.why_it_matters: SQL is a core skill for many data, analytics, and business intelligence roles. If a job asks for SQL, recruiters want to see proof that you can query, filter, join, and summarize structured data.
action_plan.suggested_project: Create a small SQL project using a public dataset. Write queries that answer business questions, then summarize the results in a short README.

ai_gap_explanation.result:
- You already have strong skills in Excel and AI, which show your analytical strength and comfort with data tools—this is a solid foundation for the role.
- Missing familiarity with business intelligence tools like PowerBI might limit your ability to create interactive reports and dashboards, which are key for this job.
- Communication skills are crucial here for teamwork, presenting findings clearly, and collaborating in a fast-paced setting.
- To improve, try learning PowerBI through free online tutorials or student projects and practice writing reports or giving presentations to build confidence.
- Joining clubs or internships where you communicate data insights can also help bridge these gaps before applying.

ai_gap_explanation.result:
- You have strong data skills with R and Excel, which is a great foundation for the analytical aspects of this role. However, familiarity with Power BI and other business intelligence tools is missing and important for creating interactive dashboards and reports.
- Communication and collaboration skills are key because the job requires working in teams and presenting insights clearly to various stakeholders; these areas could be strengthened through group projects or presentation practice.
- Attention to detail and organization matter because this role involves managing multiple tasks and ensuring accurate data interpretation; improving these through time management techniques or checklist use will help.
- To improve, engage in activities like student clubs, internships, or online courses focused on business intelligence tools and soft skills like communication and teamwork.
- Gaining experience with PowerPoint and practicing verbal presentations can also boost your readiness for the travel and client-facing aspects of the job.

action_plan.why_it_matters: Dashboarding and business intelligence skills are important for analytics roles because they show that you can turn data into something useful for decision-making.

- Communication, collaboration, presentations, and organizational skills matter for effectively sharing insights and working in team settings.
- To improve, focus on learning Power BI (many free tutorials exist) and practice soft skills through group projects, presentations, or campus activities.
- Highlight any experience managing tasks or working in teams to show your growing organizational and interpersonal abilities.

ai_gap_explanation.result:
- You have strong skills in R, Excel, and Artificial Intelligence, which align well with data analysis and AI experience valued in this role.
- The only missing technical skill is TypeScript, a programming language often used in web development, which is not directly required here.
- Since this job focuses on data analysis and supplier requalification, prioritizing mastery of Excel and data tools is more important than learning TypeScript right now.
- To improve, continue building advanced Excel skills, especially with pivot tables, and develop your ability to manage multiple tasks efficiently in fast-paced environments.
- Gaining practical experience through internships or projects involving quality data analysis will also strengthen your fit for this position.

fit_analysis.label: Strong Skills, Needs Clearer Wording
fit_analysis.summary: You already have many of the right skills for this role, but your resume could do a better job communicating them in the same language used in the job description.
fit_analysis.next_action: Try rewriting a few resume bullets using keywords and responsibilities from the posting while still keeping everything truthful to your experience.

action_plan.title: Make Your Resume Match the Role Better
action_plan.why_it_matters: Your resume may include relevant experience, but the wording does not clearly mirror the job description yet. Recruiters and screening tools need to quickly see how your background connects to the role.
action_plan.suggested_project: Rewrite 2 to 3 resume bullets using language from the job description while keeping the experience truthful. Focus on tools used, outcomes, responsibilities, and who benefited from the work.

ai_gap_explanation.result:
- You have useful skills in R, Excel (including Pivot tables), and Artificial Intelligence, which align well with the job’s requirements and preferred experience.
- The main gap is TypeScript, a programming language often used for web development and automation; lacking this may limit your ability to contribute to certain projects or team workflows that use it.
- TypeScript skills matter here because they can enhance your versatility in handling software tools or scripts that support data tasks and business applications.
- To improve, consider taking an online beginner course or tutorial on TypeScript to build foundational knowledge; even a small project or practice exercises can boost your confidence.
- Highlight your strong multitasking ability, progress in your STEM degree, and your existing tech skills to show you’re eager and capable of learning necessary new tools quickly.

ai_gap_explanation.result:
- You have strong skills in R, Excel, and AI, which align well with data analysis and automation tasks mentioned in the job. This shows you can handle technical aspects effectively.  
- The gap is in TypeScript, a programming language often used for web development and interactive tools. This skill matters if the role involves building or maintaining web interfaces, which complements data tasks.  
- Since you're early in your career, focusing on learning TypeScript through online courses or small projects can quickly boost your versatility and make you more competitive.  
- Your ability to prioritize tasks and use advanced Excel functions already matches key job requirements, so highlight these strengths alongside your willingness to learn new tools.  
- Consider joining coding clubs or internships that offer exposure to TypeScript and fast-paced environments to practice multitasking and real-world application.
